evals.prompt: I'm new to building Workfront UI extensions on Adobe App Builder. Where do I start, and what does the whole process look like?
evals.expected_output: An end-to-end roadmap (set up + create project -> scaffold -> build front end + Runtime actions -> test in Workfront -> deploy -> publish) that routes each stage to the right sub-skill.

assertions.description: Routes front-end work to the UI extension skill.
Routes back-end work to the actions skill.
Routes testing to the local-testing skill.

evals.prompt: How do the front end and back end of a Workfront App Builder extension fit together, and what's the golden rule about API calls?
evals.expected_output: Explains the two halves (React/Spectrum SPA + serverless Runtime actions) and that the browser never calls Workfront/Adobe APIs directly, only through an action via actionWebInvoke.

assertions.description: States the browser must never call Workfront/Adobe APIs directly.

evals.prompt: What are the non-negotiable invariants I must follow when building a Workfront App Builder extension?
evals.expected_output: Lists the family invariants: auth from sharedContext (no custom login), no direct API calls from the browser, CommonJS actions, inputs via params not process.env, Node 20, Workfront Public API v21.

assertions.description: Auth comes from the shared context.
Warns not to read process.env at runtime.
